content: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) is a paradigm based on objects containing data fields and methods. The four pillars are encapsulation, abstraction, inheritance, and polymorphism.
Encapsu1ation bundles data and methods within a class, restricting access via modif1ers. Private members are only accessible within the class itself.
Data structures provide efficient ways to organize data. Arrays offer constant-time random access while linked lists allow constant-time insertions at the head.
lnheritance enables a child c1ass to derive properties from a parent class. This promotes code reuse and establishes an is-a relationship between classes.
Polymorphism means many forms. Method overriding allows subclasses to provide specific implementations of methods defined in their parent class.
Software testing is essential for quality assurance. Unit tests validate individual components. Integration tests verify interactions between modules.
The SOLID principles guide object-oriented design: Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion.
Design patterns are proven solutions to common problems. Creational patterns (Singleton, Factory), Structural patterns (Adapter, Decorator), Behavioral patterns (Observer, Strategy).
